description_long: This bollard consists of a series of rectangular wooden blocks arranged in a linear fashion, with two blocks stacked vertically at one end and three blocks positioned at an angle, appearing to have toppled or leaned against each other. The wood has a natural, light brown finish with visible grain patterns, giving it a smooth yet organic appearance. The edges of each block are slightly rounded, and the surfaces are flat and even, suggesting careful craftsmanship. There are no visible fasteners, markings, or decorative elements, and the overall design is minimalist and utilitarian. The bollard appears solid and robust, likely intended to serve as a physical barrier or demarcation in an urban environment. Its modular, blocky construction makes it visually distinct from traditional cylindrical or metal bollards.
description: A minimalist bollard made of light brown wooden blocks, with two stacked vertically and three angled as if toppled. The design is simple, robust, and features smooth, flat surfaces with rounded edges.
description_view_0: A narrow side view showing the stacked vertical blocks, emphasizing the slim profile and smooth wooden surfaces.
description_view_1: A perspective view displaying the full arrangement: two upright blocks and three angled blocks, highlighting the modular, toppled design.
description_view_2: A direct end view showing the linear alignment of the blocks, with the vertical stack in the foreground and the angled blocks receding.
description_view_3: A wide view from the opposite side, clearly showing the two upright blocks and the three angled blocks, emphasizing the staggered, toppled arrangement.
